commit.message:
Rename package from com.notification to org.notification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Grafana dashboard by setting datasource UID and enabling histogram metrics

Dashboard panels referenced datasource uid "prometheus" but the
provisioned datasource had no explicit UID. Also enabled percentiles
histogram for HTTP server requests so p95/p99 latency panels have data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Redis cache serialization by using JSON instead of JDK serializer

The default JdkSerializationRedisSerializer requires cached objects to
implement Serializable. Entity classes (User, Device, NotificationTemplate)
don't, causing every @Cacheable call to fail with SerializationException
and resulting in 100% load test failures. Switch to
GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer. Also promote load-client error
logging from DEBUG to ERROR for better visibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace wildcard imports with explicit single-class imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add healthcheck to notification-server for load-client readiness

The load-client was starting before the notification-server Spring Boot
app was ready, causing 100% request failures. Use actuator health
endpoint as healthcheck and require service_healthy condition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Docker build by generating per-service settings.gradle

Each Dockerfile was copying the shared settings.gradle which references
all subprojects, but only one subproject's source was available during
build. Generate a minimal settings.gradle with only the target subproject.

commit.message:
chore: CI commits only SVG charts, not README

README.md was being committed by CI on every push, causing daily rebase
conflicts for local development. CI now commits only benchmark/charts/*.svg
(files users never edit manually) so pulls are always clean.

The numbers table is removed from the static README — the charts show
the same information more clearly and stay fresh automatically.

Add Rust cache to pre-commit CI job (#213)

the cargo-test, coverage, and benchmarks jobs

used by cargo-check, clippy, cargo-fmt, and cargo-sort hooks

Fix modal title alignment: put title before close button

the title puts the title on the left and the X on the right.

  is_ssh_transport pub — enables downstream crates to use forjar transport
- 3 new kernel header validation tests, total 11 new tests

Refs PMAT-053

commit.message:
feat(staff-engineer): enforce kanban discipline for built-in agents, add data-driven estimation

Encodes lessons from a coordination mistake where the Explore agent was used
outside the kanban workflow and misrepresented as "Researcher." Adds clear
rules for built-in Agent types, Plan mode vs project-planner boundaries, and
agent-scale timeline calibration. Introduces `claude-inspect estimate` and
`throughput` CLI commands so estimates are grounded in historical P50/P75/P90
data rather than human-scale guesses.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: cluster arrangement force — spiral, concentric, tree, grid patterns

Add cluster arrangement system that positions nodes according to
selected patterns (Archimedean spiral, concentric rings, BFS tree,
m×n grid). Works with or without group-by selection — when groupBy
is "none", all nodes are treated as a single group.

Key changes:
- New cluster-force.ts with buildClusterForce() using absolute
  position blending + velocity kill to override d3 forces
- applyClusterForce() suppresses charge/center/link/directionalGravity/
  enclosureRepulsion when cluster is active, restores on deactivation
- Panel UI: cluster section with groupBy/arrangement/strength controls
- "力の強さ" section hidden when cluster arrangement is active
- 31 unit tests including d3 simulation pipeline integration tests
- E2E visual test infrastructure with Playwright screenshots

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add homelab expert skill portfolio entry

Documents the homelab advisory skill: same subagent-to-skill origin as the
travel agent, but the key finding here is different — the skill encodes a
reasoning framework (decision filters, structured threat model, hard constraint
doctrine) rather than just domain facts. Covers the architectural decisions
behind formatting the threat model as tabular data and treating constraints as
non-negotiable red lines rather than preferences.

commit.message:
feat: add --setup mode to run_whatsapp_monitor for headless QR auth

--setup opens WhatsApp Web headlessly, screenshots the QR code to
/tmp/wa_qr.png (configurable via --qr-path), waits for scan, saves
the session, then exits. Normal --monitor runs fail fast if not authed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: bulletproof JSON parser for AI topology responses

Replace all inline JSON parsing with shared extractJSON() and
parseAndMergeTopology() functions that handle every possible format:
- Markdown code fences (```json ... ```) anywhere in text
- Leading/trailing prose text around JSON
- Top-level arrays [ {page1}, {page2} ]
- Top-level objects { pages: [...] }
- Truncated responses (finds matching bracket by nesting depth)
- Proper string-aware bracket matching (ignores brackets inside strings)

Single source of truth used in all 3 parse paths (service + 2 controller endpoints).

Fix: Prevent price fetching from blocking the event loop

- Wrap all blocking market data service calls with run_in_threadpool so
  FastAPI's event loop stays free while waiting on Zerodha/RapidAPI responses
- Refactor get_batch_prices to fetch symbols concurrently using
  ThreadPoolExecutor instead of sequentially in a loop

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add derivatives module + EGX ETP ingestion pipeline

Derivatives module (derivatives/):
- BSMPricer: Black-Scholes-Merton with GARCH/regime/vol-surface hooks
- BinomialPricer: CRR tree for American options with early-exercise boundary
- MCDerivativesPricer: Asian, Barrier (BGK correction), Lookback via existing MC paths
- VolSurface: SVI parametric fit per slice with no-arbitrage + calendar-spread enforcement
- AnalyticalGreeks + SecondOrderGreeks (Vanna, Volga, Charm, Veta, Speed, Color)
- Numerical Greeks: finite-difference with adaptive epsilon
- PortfolioGreeks: multi-position aggregation + delta-hedge ratios
- DeltaHedgeSimulator: full path-based delta-hedging P&L with transaction costs
- OptionsVaR: delta-gamma approx + full revaluation + 4 stress scenarios
- DerivativesLogger: SHA-256 hash-chained audit trail (standalone verify_chain)
- 98/98 tests passing (pytest derivatives/tests/)

Integration stubs (simulation/, audit/, risk/):
- SimulationResult dataclass + get_current_garch_vol()
- AuditLogger with hash chain + get_latest_hash()
- PortfolioVaR + VaRResult + get_scenario_pnl_distribution()

EGX ETP ingestion (xmore/egx_etp/):
- Discovers full ETP universe from egx.com.eg (ETFSPr, FundConstituents, NavUnit, EtfFundVolume)
- Conservative classification: ETF, GOLD_ETP, INDEX_TRACKER, STRUCTURED_NOTE, ETN, UNKNOWN_ETP
- requests → Playwright fallback for JS-blocked pages
- PostgreSQL upserts (SQLite fallback for local dev)
- Daily incremental + one-off backfill scripts
- Cron: 30 12 * * 0-4 (14:30 Cairo, after EGX closes)

commit.message:
fix: #63 StopOnFirst skips dependents instead of halting everything

StopOnFirst policy now tracks failed resource IDs and cascades failure
only to resources whose depends_on includes a failed resource. Independent
resources continue executing normally. This prevents a single failure
(e.g. bin-copia SEC010) from blocking unrelated resources like earlyoom,
sysctl, ufw, etc.

Changes:
- MachineCounters: add failed_resources HashSet + failed_dependency() helper
- execute_sequential: check depends_on before apply, skip with JIDOKA message
- execute_single_wave: same dependency check for single-resource waves
- execute_wave_parallel: filter dep-failed resources before wave execution
- record_wave_outcomes: track failures in failed_resources, don't stop between waves
- record_failure: update JIDOKA message to "dependents will be skipped"

Refs FJ-63

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: hasRealCredentials always evaluated to false when credentials were set

TEST_USER.email reads from process.env.E2E_TEST_EMAIL, so checking
`process.env.E2E_TEST_EMAIL !== TEST_USER.email` was always `X !== X`
— permanently false whenever the env var was set. Replaced with hardcoded
placeholder strings so the check correctly returns true for real credentials.

Affected: auth.spec.ts, challenges.spec.ts, community.spec.ts

feat: ラスト15秒パニック演出 + 正解ボーナス+5秒タイム回復を追加

- body.panic .timer:not(.bonus) → timerPanicPulse で赤点滅ループ
- BGM加速: <20s→280ms下限, <15s→180ms下限でさらに加速
- 正解時(残り≤15s)に bonusMs +5000ms を Firebase 同期 → 両プレイヤーのタイマー即時反映
- 回復演出: .timer.bonus アニメーション(緑グロー)+ "+5 SEC!" フロートテキスト
- 回復音: 880Hz→1320Hz の2音上昇(Web Audio API)
- ボーナス後に残り>15sになればパニックモード自動解除（再突入も対応）
- phase1(PIN正解)/phase2(ワイヤー正解)/phase3(シンボル正解)すべてに対応
- enterFinalArm/enterReview で exitPanicMode() を呼び確実にクリーンアップ

fix: use map_location= in GLiNER.from_pretrained to avoid meta-tensor error

Calling .to(device) after from_pretrained() fails with 'Cannot copy out
of meta tensor' when GLiNER initialises weights on a meta device internally.
GLiNER 0.2.x exposes map_location= in from_pretrained() which loads weights
directly onto the target device, bypassing the issue entirely.

commit.message:
fix: block Google Ads phones in ENC24 resolver, scope early WA to contactBox

Root cause: the network interceptor was capturing phone numbers from
Google DoubleClick ad responses (which include "encuentra24.com" in their
query params, passing the URL filter). These fake ad-network phones were
returned as "seller phones" via the net_candidate_phone path.

Changes:
- Block doubleclick.net, googlesyndication, googleads, pagead, adsense,
  facebook.com/tr, and analytics URLs in the network response handler
- Scope early_wa_check to contactBox only (was page-wide, capturing
  Encuentra24's own site-level WhatsApp button instead of seller's)
- Add early_wa contactBox fallback before net candidates (path C2)
- Expand dealer name tokens: galería, escudería, grupo, empresa, etc.
- Tighten toPanamaE164 to reject non-Panama numbers
- Add isSuspiciousPhone for mobile-only validation (+507 6xxx)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Security audit: secret scan, dependency check, env hardening

Task 1 — Secret scan:
- No real secrets found in git history or src/
- .env.example contains only placeholder values; sk-ant-your-key-here is NOT a real key
- Zod validator for ANTHROPIC_API_KEY prefix (sk-ant-) is expected and safe

Task 2 — Dependency audit (0 vulnerabilities remaining):
- playwright 1.49.1 → 1.58.2 (fixes HIGH SSL cert bypass GHSA-7mvr-c777-76hp)
- tsx 4.19.2 → 4.21.0 (ships esbuild ~0.27.0, fixes MODERATE dev-server CORS GHSA-67mh-4wv8-2f99)
- @supabase/supabase-js 2.47.12 → 2.58.0 (includes auth-js 2.72.0, fixes LOW GHSA-8r88-6cj9-9fh5)

Task 3 — Code review findings (no code changes needed):
- All entry points call load*Env() first — Zod validates every secret before use ✅
- Claude API output validated via extractedOpportunitySchema before any DB write ✅
- Raw process.env in extractor.ts, telegram-distributor.ts, kit-client.ts is redundant
  (entry points already validated) — documented pattern, same as telegram.ts ✅
- extraction-schema.ts: enum guards for type/region/target_audience, date regex+parse
  validation, URL .catch(null) fallback — all scraped data fully sanitized before DB ✅

Task 4 — .env.example hardening:
- Add missing TELEGRAM_PUBLIC_CHANNEL_ID (distribute-telegram.yml)
- Add missing KIT_API_SECRET (weekly-digest.yml)
- Add inline comments mapping each var to its workflow

Task 5 — .gitignore: .env, storage/, node_modules/ all present ✅

Fix realtime auth: use SSR client in global-view, open SELECT policy

- global-view.ts: switch from raw @supabase/supabase-js createClient
  (reads localStorage, no session) to SSR createBrowserClient (reads
  cookies, has the user's auth session) so realtime payload passes RLS
- SQL: change SELECT policy from auth.role()='authenticated' to
  USING(true) so subscribers without a session also get full payload
